content.sentence: Món ăn này có ___ gia vị.

translation.en: This dish has too much seasoning.

notes_md:
**thừa** = extra, more than needed, leftover. It describes something that is beyond what’s necessary.

It usually comes **after a noun**: *cơm thừa, tiền thừa, thời gian thừa*. It can also be the main adjective in a sentence to say something is excessive.

`Structure: còn thừa + noun (còn thừa tiền)`

Very close to **dư** (also “extra”); opposite is **thiếu** (not enough). It can also mean “unnecessary” in some contexts (ý kiến thừa = unnecessary opinion).

translation.en: I feel extra at the party.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ trong bữa tiệc.

exampleSentencesTranslated: If you cook extra rice, I can take it to work tomorrow.
She has an extra shirt, do you want to take it?
After the party, there is a lot of extra cake on the table.

example.en: I have extra time to finish the assignment.
example.es: Tengo tiempo de sobra para terminar la tarea.
example.vi: Tôi có thừa thời gian để hoàn thành bài tập.

definitions.en: Excess or surplus; more than what is needed.
definitions.vi: Sự dư thừa; nhiều hơn những gì cần thiết.
definitions.es: Exceso o sobrante; más de lo que se necesita.

notes:
thừa = extra, more than needed, leftover. It describes something that is beyond what’s necessary.

It usually comes after a noun: cơm thừa, tiền thừa, thời gian thừa. It can also be the main adjective in a sentence to say something is excessive.

Structure: còn thừa + noun (còn thừa tiền)

Very close to dư (also “extra”); opposite is thiếu (not enough). It can also mean “unnecessary” in some contexts (ý kiến thừa = unnecessary opinion).
exampleSentences: Nếu em nấu cơm thừa, anh có thể mang đi làm ngày mai.
Chị có một cái áo thừa, em có muốn lấy không?
Sau bữa tiệc, còn rất nhiều bánh thừa trên bàn.
